initial_access_vector: Attacker obtained credentials for IAM user DevOps1-bpvbp (no MFA) and logged into the AWS Management Console from IP 185.195.232.111
persistence_mechanism: Creation of a new IAM super user account named DevOps3 via the AWS Management Console for later reuse
privilege_escalation: sudo to switch from ssm-user to ec2-user on the EC2 host after initiating an SSM session

techniques.evidence_quote: we can see that this user did not have MFA enabled. We can also see the source IP address from where the user logged in on the console
The first event we find related to the user is the CreateUser event ... a new super user account was found

techniques.technique_name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell
techniques.evidence_quote: switched user to the default Amazon Linux2 account ec2-user which initiated a new bash session
We can see the timings for the ssh connection line up with the findings from our analysis of i-08e56d6b6c0439daf
a StartSession event was recorded ... a session was started from the account DevOps1-bpvbp towards an instance
the aws command line tool to provide detailed information of the security group that the instance belongs to, in particular looking for port 22 entries

techniques.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
techniques.evidence_quote: the Instance MetaData Service (IMDS) being used to extract information regarding the Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) Id
looking for port 22 entries ... a method to perform network discovery to find other hosts that can be used for lateral movement
a database table was dumped to a file named staff.txt
the attacker was very much interested in information from the staff table in the postgres database
the aws command line to create a S3 bucket and the file staff.txt was copied to it. This could be seen as staging data for exfiltration

attack_chain.description: Attacker logged into the AWS Management Console as IAM user DevOps1-bpvbp (no MFA enabled) from source IP 185.195.232.111, approximately 6 minutes before creating a new account.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: we see the following event 6 minutes prior to the creation of the new account ... this user did not have MFA enabled ... IP-address used: 185.195.232.111

attack_chain.description: Using DevOps1-bpvbp, the attacker created a new IAM super user account named DevOps3 via the AWS Management Console (CreateUser event) as a persistence mechanism.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Creation Time: 26–10–2022 at 21:29:03 ... Created by: DevOps1-bpvbp ... Creation method: AWS management console

attack_chain.description: Attacker initiated an AWS Systems Manager StartSession from DevOps1-bpvbp to EC2 instance i-08e56d6b6c0439daf, opening an interactive SSM session on the host.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a StartSession event was recorded ... a session was started from the account DevOps1-bpvbp towards an instance with identifier i-08e56d6b6c0439daf

attack_chain.technique_name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell
attack_chain.description: On the EC2 instance, ssm-user executed sudo to switch to ec2-user and started a bash session to run commands interactively.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the associated ssm-user switched user to the default Amazon Linux2 account ec2-user which initiated a new bash session

evidence_chunks.quote: the associated ssm-user switched user to the default Amazon Linux2 account ec2-user which initiated a new bash session
the associated ssm-user switched user to the default Amazon Linux2 account ec2-user which initiated a new bash session

attack_chain.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
attack_chain.description: Attacker queried the EC2 Instance Metadata Service (IMDS) to retrieve VPC information about the host.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the Instance MetaData Service (IMDS) being used to extract information regarding the Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) Id

evidence_chunks.quote: the Instance MetaData Service (IMDS) being used to extract information regarding the Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) Id
the Instance MetaData Service (IMDS) being used to extract information regarding the Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) Id

attack_chain.description: Attacker used the aws CLI to enumerate security groups associated with the VPC and filter for port 22 rules to find SSH-accessible hosts for lateral movement.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The VPC Id is then used in conjunction with the aws command line tool to provide detailed information of the security group that the instance belongs to, in particular looking for port 22 entries

evidence_chunks.quote: The VPC Id is then used in conjunction with the aws command line tool to provide detailed information of the security group that the instance belongs to, in particular looking for port 22 entries
The VPC Id is then used in conjunction with the aws command line tool to provide detailed information of the security group that the instance belongs to, in particular looking for port 22 entries

attack_chain.description: Attacker SSH'd from i-08e56d6b6c0439daf to a second EC2 instance (10.0.8.25 / i-020e722274233c7f2) for lateral movement.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the timings for the ssh connection line up with the findings from our analysis of i-08e56d6b6c0439daf

attack_chain.description: On i-020e722274233c7f2 the attacker queried the PostgreSQL database and dumped the staff table to /tmp/staff.txt.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a database table was dumped to a file named staff.txt ... attacker was very much interested in information from the staff table in the postgres database

attack_chain.description: Attacker used scp to copy staff.txt from i-020e722274233c7f2 back to i-08e56d6b6c0439daf (same SHA256 hash confirmed on both hosts).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: copied the database dump file staff.txt file from i-020e722274233c7f2 to i-08e56d6b6c0439daf

attack_chain.description: Attacker used the aws CLI to create a new S3 bucket and uploaded staff.txt to it, staging/exfiltrating the stolen data to an attacker-controlled cloud location.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the aws command line to create a S3 bucket and the file staff.txt was copied to it. This could be seen as staging data for exfiltration

evidence_chunks.quote: the aws command line to create a S3 bucket and the file staff.txt was copied to it. This could be seen as staging data for exfiltration
the aws command line to create a S3 bucket and the file staff.txt was copied to it. This could be seen as staging data for exfiltration
